module_map.responsibility: Orchestrates the full pipeline: initializes Icons/Colors/GitTheme, fetches metadata via Meta::from_path, sorts via sort module, and delegates to display module for output.

Aggregates file metadata from filesystem. Meta struct holds name, path, permissions, date, owner, size, symlink, inode, links, content (recursive), access_control, git_status. Submodules extract individual fields.

Formats Meta objects into grid/tree output. Key function get_output extracts cell data per Block. Uses Block enum to determine which fields to display, applies Colors and Icons, handles column alignment via padding_rules.

Defines Block enum (Permission, User, Group, Size, Date, Name, INode, Links, GitStatus, etc.). Blocks struct manages ordered vec of blocks to display. configure_from method builds from Cli flags and Config.
Command-line flag parsing and configuration. 16 submodules (color, date, icons, sorting, display, layout, etc.) each handle one CLI option. Flags struct aggregates all.
Theme configuration and lookup. color.rs maps file attributes to terminal colors; icon.rs maps file types to glyphs. Loaded from config files and queried by display module.
Sorting logic. SortFn is a function pointer type. by_meta sorts Meta objects using sorters vec built from Flags. Delegates recursively to Meta.content.

data_flow.key_function: Meta::from_path(path: &Path, dereference: bool, permission_flag: PermissionFlag) -> io::Result<Meta>
data_flow.description: Reads filesystem metadata (stat, permissions, date, owner, symlink target, etc.) and populates Meta struct. Called once per file in core::fetch.
types_produced: Meta with name, path, permissions_or_attributes, date, owner, file_type, size, symlink, indicator, inode, links, access_control (git_status added later from GitCache)

data_flow.key_function: Meta::recurse_into(depth: usize, flags: &Flags, cache: Option<&GitCache>) -> io::Result<(Option<Vec<Meta>>, ExitCode)>
data_flow.description: If directory and recursion enabled, recursively calls from_path on children, stores in Meta.content vec. GitCache queries are performed at this stage.
types_produced: Vec<Meta> stored in parent Meta.content; git_status Option<GitFileStatus> added to Meta

data_flow.key_function: Core::sort(&self, metas: &mut Vec<Meta>) calls sort::by_meta(sorters, a, b); recursively sorts Meta.content
data_flow.description: Sorts metas vector by SortFn closures built from Flags. Each sorter compares Meta fields (name, size, date, etc.). Applied recursively to all depth levels.
types_produced: Vec<Meta> sorted in-place; no new types

data_flow.key_function: display::grid(metas: &[Meta], flags: &Flags, colors: &Colors, icons: &Icons, git_theme: &GitTheme) -> String
data_flow.description: Delegates to inner_display_grid which iterates metas, calls get_output per Meta to build Vec<String> cells, applies padding_rules, renders via Grid library.

data_flow.key_function: get_output(meta: &Meta, owner_cache: &OwnerCache, colors: &Colors, icons: &Icons, git_theme: &GitTheme, flags: &Flags, display_option: &DisplayOption, padding_rules: &HashMap<Block, usize>, tree: (usize, &str)) -> Vec<String>
data_flow.description: For each Block in Flags.blocks, extracts corresponding field from Meta (name → meta.name.render(), size → meta.size.render(), etc.), applies icon/color, pads to alignment. Returns vec of strings joined into row.
types_produced: Vec<String> (one entry per Block, formatted with colors/icons)

data_flow.key_function: Icons::get(file_type: &FileType, name: &str) lookup into icons theme; meta::name::Name::render(&self, colors: &Colors, icons: &Icons, ...) applies icon
data_flow.description: During get_output, Name::render queries Icons theme based on FileType and filename pattern. Returns glyph string. Colors applied separately.

data_flow.key_function: Colors::get_file_color(file_type: &FileType, name: &str, permissions: ...) or similar; applied in get_output via Elem enum
data_flow.description: During get_output, field strings are wrapped with ANSI color codes. color::Elem enum defines categories (Foreground, Background). Applied based on FileType, Name, and Permissions.

data_flow.key_function: Core::display(&self, metas: &[Meta]) calls display::grid or display::tree, prints result
data_flow.description: Final formatted string from display module printed to stdout.

cross_module_hubs.reason: Depends on color (Colors, Elem), icon (Icons), git_theme (GitTheme), meta (Meta, FileType, Name, OwnerCache, DisplayOption), flags (Block, Display, HyperlinkOption, Layout, Flags). Called by core.rs. Central output formatter.

Depends on flags (Flags, PermissionFlag, Layout, Display), git (GitCache), meta submodules (Name, Date, Owner, FileType, Size, SymLink, Indicator, INode, Links, AccessControl, GitFileStatus). Called by core::fetch. Metadata aggregator.
Depends on display, meta, flags, color, icon, git_theme, sort, git. Called by main.rs. Orchestrator that ties all modules together into the pipeline.
Imported by display.rs and core.rs. Defines Block enum used to determine what fields to render. Configuration hub for output structure.

change_proposal.title: Add SHA-256 Checksum Display Column
files_to_modify: src/flags/blocks.rs - Add Block::Checksum variant and get_header() case
src/meta/mod.rs - Add checksum: Option<String> field to Meta struct; compute in from_path() via sha256 crate or std::fs
src/display.rs - Add Checksum case in get_output() to render meta.checksum field
src/flags/display.rs or src/app.rs - Add --checksum or --blocklist option to include Checksum block
Cargo.toml - Add sha2 or sha256 crate for hashing
new_types: Checksum flag struct (similar to SizeFlag, INode flag) to gate --checksum
No new Meta field types needed; String is sufficient for hex digest
change_proposal.pattern_to_follow: Follow the Size block pattern: (1) Add Block::Size variant in blocks.rs with get_header(). (2) Add Option<Size> field to Meta in meta/mod.rs. (3) In Meta::from_path, call Size::from_path(...) or similar. (4) In display::get_output, match Block::Size and call meta.size.render(...). (5) Checksum matches this 1-to-1 except compute happens via sha2::digest(fs::read(&path)) instead of stat().
change_proposal.integration_point: In get_output, when Block::Checksum is encountered, compute digest on-the-fly (with caching via OwnerCache-style mechanism recommended) or pre-compute in from_path. Add to padding_rules for column width. Colors can be applied uniformly (no semantic color needed; use default or file color). Icons not applicable.
risks: Performance: SHA-256 hashing large files blocks the thread. Recommend pre-computing during from_path phase and storing in Meta. Consider rayon parallelism.
Recursive overhead: Computing checksum for thousands of files in tree layout scales poorly. May want to make --checksum mutually exclusive with deep --depth.
Cache coherence: If files change during execution, checksums become stale. lsd is a snapshot tool; acceptable risk.
Block layout: Adding Checksum increases row width. Terminal width constraints may truncate. Test with --terminal-width edge cases.
Configuration: Decide if Checksum is part of long format (–l) by default, or opt-in only. Recommend opt-in to preserve --l output backwards compatibility.

tool_usage.purpose: High-level file tree with function/class counts; identified core.rs, display.rs, meta/mod.rs as key modules
Functions (from_path, recurse_into, calculate_total_size) and Meta struct fields; identified metadata collection phase
Functions (grid, inner_display_grid, get_output); identified output formatting phase and Block-to-Meta mapping
Core struct and pipeline functions (fetch, sort, display); identified orchestration and data flow phases
Located files containing key functions across modules
Identified Block enum variants (Permission, User, Group, Size, Date, Name, INode, Links, GitStatus)
Extracted Meta struct fields confirming data aggregation pattern
Identified display.rs imports (color, flags/blocks, icons, git_theme); confirmed hub status
Traced pipeline: fetch (from_path) → recurse_into → sort → display
Confirmed Block enum pattern and get_header() method used in display
